title: Cayratia acris
taxonomy: Cayratia acris is classified in the plant kingdom, specifically within the family Vitaceae. This family is commonly known as the grape or Vitaceae family, which consists of around 14 to 16 genera. Cayratia acris is further classified under the genus Cayratia, which includes around 70 species of flowering plants. The genus Cayratia belongs to the order Vitales, which is a group of flowering plants known for their simple, alternate leaves and small, often inconspicuous flowers. Cayratia acris is also placed in the class Magnoliopsida, which includes flowering plants that have two cotyledons in their seedlings and net-veined leaves.
uses: Cayratia acris, a plant species found in various parts of Asia, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits. Its leaves and stems are said to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating wounds and skin infections. The plant is also believed to have antipyretic properties, making it a potential treatment for fever. Additionally, Cayratia acris has been studied for its antioxidant properties, which may help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Cayratia acris as a medicinal remedy.

parts: Cayratia acris is a plant species that has been found to have medicinal properties, but specific information on its medicinal parts is not readily available. preparations: Cayratia acris is reportedly used in traditional medicine, and herbal preparations often incorporate its leaves and other plant parts for various health benefits. intro: Cayratia acris, also known as Indian creeper, is a perennial climber native to tropical Asia, belonging to the family Vitaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Cayratia acris include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are consumed as herbal teas or used as topical applications to alleviate various health conditions.
